_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to create our own texts by adapting plot structures, characters, settings and ideas from stories we have read, experimenting with our own style.

_source.w: We are learning to create our own texts by adapting plot structures, characters, settings and ideas from stories we have read, experimenting with our own style.
y: Adapting from published texts is how writers learn their craft and develop their own voice.
Experimentation with style helps us discover what kind of writers we want to be.
This creative work draws together all our reading and analytical skills.
eg: We adapt a familiar plot structure and set it in a completely new context.
We create characters inspired by literature but with unique traits and motivations.
We experiment with our writing style — trying different sentence lengths, tones and voices.
We write a story that references or responds to a text we studied.
We develop our own distinctive writing voice through deliberate experimentation.
We seek feedback on our creative work and revise it to strengthen our style choices.
